title: DOCKER Container Port Mapping Validator with Duplicate Service Names

statement.markdown: Given a list of Docker container port mappings, validate that there are no duplicate service names and all port mappings are valid. A port mapping is valid if it is of the form `<port>/udp` or `<port>/tcp` and the port number is between 1 and 65535. If the input is valid, return `true`; otherwise, return `false`.
statement.inputFormat: A list of strings representing Docker container port mappings in the format `<service_name>:<port>/<protocol>`. For example: `['service1:80/tcp', 'service2:443/udp', 'service1:80/tcp']`.
statement.outputFormat: A boolean indicating whether the input is valid.
statement.notes: The service names must be unique, and the port numbers must be between 1 and 65535.

examples.explanation: All service names are unique, and all port mappings are valid.
The service name 'service1' appears twice, so the input is invalid.
The port number 123456 is greater than 65535, so the input is invalid.

hints.text: Use a Set to keep track of unique service names

editorial.title: DOCKER Container Port Mapping Validator with Duplicate Service Names
editorial.intuition: You can use a Set to keep track of unique service names and check for duplicate service names in O(n) time.
editorial.bruteForce: You can iterate over each port mapping and check if the service name exists in the Set. If it does, return false. Otherwise, add the service name to the Set and continue to the next port mapping.
editorial.betterSolution: You can use a Set to keep track of unique service names and check for invalid port numbers in O(n) time.
editorial.optimalSolution: You can use a Set to keep track of unique service names and check for invalid port numbers in O(n) time. You can use regular expressions to parse the port number and protocol.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The solution is correct because it checks for both duplicate service names and invalid port numbers.
commonMistakes: Not checking for invalid port numbers
Not using a Set to keep track of unique service names

starterCode.javascript: // Function to validate Docker container port mappings
